§6Entropy Manipulator§f:
The §6Entropy Manipulator §f(appliedenergistics2:entropy_manipulator) is a §6powered tool §f that §brotates, dismantles, and configures §f AE2 blocks. It is the §6primary maintenance tool §f for an AE2 network.

§6Production Path (tools/misctools_entropy_manipulator.json)§f:
Pattern "ab / cd /  d" (an asymmetric 3x3 pattern):
- §ba§f: §6Fluix Crystal §f(appliedenergistics2:material:7 or any crystal in the §6#appliedenergistics2:fluixCrystal §foredict tag) — 1 (top left)
- §bb§f: §6ME Energy Cell §f(appliedenergistics2:energy_cell) — 1 (top right)
- §cc§f: §6Engineering Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:24) — 1 (middle left)
- §cd§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 3 (middle right + bottom)
- Yields 1× Entropy Manipulator

§6Function§f:
- §bBlock rotation §f: right-click an AE2 block to rotate it (Controller, Drive, Chest, etc.)
- §bBlock disassembly §f: sneak-right-click to dismantle the block and §bretrieve its components §f
- §bConfiguration §f: right-click a §6Security Terminal §f to cycle permissions
- §bInternal energy §f: stored in its own §6internal §f §b400,000 AE §f buffer (1/4 of an Energy Cell)
- §bPower cost §f: §b40 AE §f per right-click, §b0 §f for sneak-right-click
- §bCharging §f: place it in a §6Charger §f to refill its internal buffer

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Entropy Manipulator is a component of, but it is referenced in 14 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bSetup and teardown §f: §6mandatory §f for any non-trivial AE2 build
- §bBlock inspection §f: sneak-right-click to see a §6block's settings §f
- §bCable rerouting §f: dismantle cables without losing §bchannels §f

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: keep a §6spare Entropy Manipulator §f in a §6ME Chest §f for emergency network work
- §eTip§f: pair with a §6Memory Card §f to §6save and restore §f block settings
- §cWarning§f: the Entropy Manipulator §cdrains AE §f from its internal buffer; it is §cnot §f a §6FE/Tesla §f tool
- §cWarning§f: dismantling a §6Controller §f or §6Quantum Ring §f will §cdestroy §f the multiblock structure
